sourceMovie.title: Trespass
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Trespass for the same reason it broke through in 2011: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Joel Schumacher keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: The Usual Suspects
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Trespass clicked for you, The Usual Suspects (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Mr. & Mrs. Smith
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Trespass clicked for you, Mr. & Mrs. Smith (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Trespass?
faq.answer: Try The Usual Suspects, Mr. & Mrs. Smith, Don't Breathe—each shares DNA with Trespass while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Trespass?
faq.answer: Trespass (2011) is best described as Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Trespass worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
